Combined Interface

Merritt Wang (S2887338) · Ch.4 “Combined Interface” deliverable
Author selector and cross-view linking are live. Radar, bar-code, small multiples, network and linear all re-render from the shared author selection below using the same client-side computation as the scale-exploration tools. Click an author's shape/row/panel in a Fingerprints view, then switch to Topology, to see their places highlighted there (and vice-versa is not yet built). The metro map is the one exception — its lines depend on offline community detection, so it swaps between precomputed 5- and 20-author snapshots rather than recomputing live.

Visualisation

Authors live

Cross-view Focus live

Click an author's shape (radar), row (bar-code) or map panel (small multiples) to focus them — then switch to Topology to see their places highlighted.

Scale Exploration

Radar — 2 to 408 authors → Network — 2 to 408 authors → ↩ back to all six (static)
Now viewing: Radar Chart

Detail Panel

planned — outer panel

A shared outer detail panel driven by api_sentence lookups is still planned. Until then, each view already has its own working hover/click detail (example sentences, book titles in the canonical six; place/mentions tooltips here) — try hovering a shape below.

Cross-view Linking

implemented

Click an author in a Fingerprints view to focus them (gold highlight). Switch to a Topology view: that author's top-15 places are highlighted gold among the current selection's combined graph, others dimmed. Clear the focus chip on the left to reset. The metro map does not participate (see note below).

Data Reuse

Radar, bar-code, small multiples, network and linear all read live from the same four scale-exploration JSON bundles (all_authors_radar.json, all_authors_barcode.json, all_authors_small_multiples.json, all_authors_network.json), covering any subset of the 408 authors with location data.
